No appearance by any other party or counsel.

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD COMPANY'S NOTICE OF DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION.

Matter called, no argumemnt held. There being no appearance by any other party or counsel, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's demurrer to plaintiff's eleventh cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for take-home exposure pursuant to Campbell v. Ford Motor Co. (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15 and is not uncertain. The facts stated demonstrate that defendant owed plaintiff a duty of care. Pursuant to Rowland v. Christian (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108 and Campbell, supra, 206 Cal.App.4th15, public policy dictates in favor of the creation of a duty in the present matter where plaintiff's father was defendant's employee. Defendant has 10 days to answer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Sandy Lee, CSR 971. (503/TLJ)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's special demurrer to plaintiff's first and second causes of action is sustained. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose the demurrer. The demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiff the opportunity to cure the defects identified by the demurrer. Specifically, plaintiff has leave to amend to allege sufficient facts to meet the standards set forth in Campbell v. Ford Motor Co., (2012) 206 Cal.App.4th 15 and Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (503/TLJ)
